title: Dual-Signature PLC Program Change Without Four-Eyes Ticket

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 2.12.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 2.12 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.26: Dual-Signature PLC Program Change Without Four-Eyes Ticket.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.26' running on index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" action="PROGRAM_CHANGE" earliest=-30d
| eval change_id=coalesce(change_ticket, work_order)
| stats values(user) as signers, dc(user) as signer_count by change_id, controller
| where signer_count<2 AND len(change_id)>4
| table change_id, controller, signers, signer_count
description: Looks for PLC program uploads/downloads signed by only one engineering identity when policy requires dual control.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_eng and sourcetypes plc:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require second approval in CM tool; (2) map PLC audit user fields; (3) integrate e-signature if available; (4) weekly compliance report. Indexes required: `index=ot_eng`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=plc:audit`.
visualization: Table, Timeline, Single value (violations). Key fields: `change_id`, `controller`, `signer_count`, `signers`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Vendor PLC audit, ServiceNow.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_eng` `sourcetype="plc:audit"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require second approval in CM tool; (2) map PLC audit user fields; (3) integrate e-signature if available; (4) weekly compliance report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" action="PROGRAM_CHANGE" earliest=-30d
| eval change_id=coalesce(change_ticket, work_order)
| stats values(user) as signers, dc(user) as signer_count by change_id, controller
| where signer_count<2 AND len(change_id)>4
| table change_id, controller, signers, signer_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Dual-Signature PLC Program Change Without Four-Eyes Ticket (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.12)** — Looks for PLC program uploads/downloads signed by only one engineering identity when policy requires dual control.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_eng` `sourcetype="plc:audit"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Vendor PLC audit, ServiceNow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_eng; **sourcetype**: plc:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_eng, sourcetype="plc:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **change_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **change_id, controller**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where signer_count<2 AND len(change_id)>4` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Output fields: change_id, controller, signer_count, signers.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline, Single value (violations).
grandmaExplanation: We looks for PLC program uploads/downloads signed by only one engineering identity when policy requires dual control. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
